【单选题】After Kobe Bryant graduated high school, he wassigned by the LA Lakers,a National Basketball Association team.  Kobe’s contract earns himmillions of dollars a year playing for the Lakers. Suppose that it is possiblefor the University of Connecticut’s (UConn) basketball coach Jim Calhoun tooffer Kobe asalary of $80,000 ayear to play for UConn and attend UConn’s undergraduate program free ofcharge.  Assuming that a collegeeducation provides no personal value, then Kobe
  A
should accept coach Calhoun’s offer because theteam would likely win the national championship.

  B
should accept coach Calhoun’s offer because the opportunity cost of playing for UConn is less than the benefit.

  C
shouldnot accept coach Calhoun’s offer because the Lakers pay him less.
  D
  shouldnot accept coach Calhoun’s offer because the opportunity cost of playing forUConn exceeds the benefit.
D should not accept coach Calhoun’s offer because the opportunity cost of playing for UConn exceeds the benefit.

【单选题】
Economist often say there is no such thing as afree lunch.  What do economists mean whenthey say such a thing?


 



  A
Economists,in this case, are talking about common resources.

  B
Someresources cause significant negative externalities.

  C
Even ifsomeone literally offers you a free lunch, there is an opportunity cost to theresources that are used to produce your free lunch.

  D
Peoplenever offer a lunch for free.
C Even if someone literally offers you a free lunch, there is an opportunity cost to the resources that are used to produce your free lunch.

An economy is a system in which resources are allocated, produced, exchanged, and distributed to fulfill the needs and wants of individuals and society. It involves the production, consumption, and trade of goods and services, as well as the distribution of wealth and income. It can be either a market economy where decisions are made by individuals and businesses interacting in the marketplace or a command economy where decisions are made by a centralized authority. The study of economics involves analyzing how economies work and how people make choices about producing, consuming, and distributing goods and services in different economic systems.

【简答题】
You win $100 ina basketball pool. You have a choice between spending the money now or putting it away for a year in a bank account that pays 5 percent interest.

What is the opportunity cost of spending the $100 now?

The opportunity cost of spending the $100 now is the amount of interest that could have been earned if it was put away in the bank account for a year. At a 5% interest rate, the amount of interest earned over a year would be $5 (5% of $100). Therefore, the opportunity cost of spending the $100 now is $5.

【资料题】
Road Performance Ltd, a tyre producer in Dandenong, Victoria, has recently signed a contract with a Japanese manufacturer to supply 10 000 motor-racing tyres to them by the end of the year. The Japanese customer is prepared to pay more than Australian buyers, so Road Performance has decided to reduce production of standard tyres for the Australian market by 10%.

The manager of Road Performance has just announced that the company will restructure the factory to cut production time for each tyre and produce a better quality product. This will mean buying new capital equipment and retrenching fifteen workers.




（1）Explain what is the basic economic problem that the Road Performance faces?

（2）What is the opportunity cost of the company’s decision signing a contract with a Japanese manufacturer to supply 10 000 motor-racing tyres to them by the end of the year?

（3）Can you give some examples of the four factors of production in Road Performance Ltd? And do you think what qualities are needed in an effective entrepreneur?

(1) The basic economic problem that Road Performance faces is the unlimited wants and needs of consumers for tyres, but the limited resources (including labor, natural resources, and capital) of the company to produce them. This requires the company to make choices regarding the production and distribution of its tyres, weighing the costs and benefits of each decision.

(2) The opportunity cost of the company's decision to sign a contract with the Japanese manufacturer to supply 10,000 motor-racing tyres is the foregone revenue or profits from reducing production of standard tyres for the Australian market by 10%. By allocating resources away from producing standard tyres for the Australian market, Road Performance is sacrificing potential revenue or profits from that market.

(3) The four factors of production in Road Performance Ltd are likely to include:

- Labor: Workers in the factory who operate the machinery and tools to produce the tyres.

- Natural resources: The rubber, chemicals, and other materials used to make the tyres.

- Capital: The machinery and equipment used to make the tyres, including any new capital equipment that the company plans to buy.

- Entrepreneurship: The managers and owners of the company who undertake the risks of producing and selling the tyres, and make decisions regarding the allocation of resources.

Regarding the qualities needed in an effective entrepreneur, some of the important qualities include:

- Vision: An effective entrepreneur needs to have a clear vision about what they want to achieve, and how to get there.

- Creativity: A willingness to challenge assumptions and think outside the box can help entrepreneurs come up with innovative solutions to problems.

- Risk-taking: Entrepreneurs need to be willing to take calculated risks, such as investing in new capital equipment or entering into a new market.

- Resilience: Starting and running a business is a challenging endeavor, and entrepreneurs need to be able to bounce back from setbacks and failures.

- Leadership: Entrepreneurs need to be able to inspire and motivate their team, and provide direction and guidance towards achieving company goals.

A market is a platform where buyers and sellers come together to exchange goods and services. It is a structure or an arrangement through which goods and services are exchanged for money or other types of compensation. In a market, buyers and sellers interact and determine the prices of goods and services based on the demand and supply factors. The level of competition in the market also impacts the prices of goods and services. Markets can be physical, such as a farmer's market or a stock exchange, or they can be virtual, such as an online marketplace. In a free-market economy, markets play a crucial role in allocating resources efficiently by enabling the forces of supply and demand to work together to determine prices.

【多选题】


Determinants that can cause a shift in a product’s demand curve are:



  A
Changes in Consumer Preference



  B
 Changes in Price



  C
Changes in Income



  D
Changes in the Price of Related Products



  E
Changes in Population

A, C, D, E. 

Changes in consumer preference, changes in income, changes in the price of related products, and changes in population are all determinants that can cause a shift in a product's demand curve. Changes in price, on the other hand, result in movements along the demand curve rather than a shift in the curve itself.

【简答题】


A decrease in the population size. The demand curve will shift to left or shift right or move along the curve?

A decrease in population size will cause a shift in the demand curve to the left. This is because with a smaller population, the number of consumers in the market for the good or service in question will decrease. Therefore, the quantity demanded at any given price will also decrease. As a result, the entire demand curve will shift to the left, indicating that consumers are willing to purchase less of the good or service at every price point.

【单选题】揑揓揖揙揕揔揘是
  A
婸揜设揘

  B
揟揠的揝揞揔揘

  C
揢揠的揝揞揔揘

  D
揤揠（揦揠）揥揝揞设揘
